published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024: efficient imputation and precision comparisons already show same event-study target need not imply same variance

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_honest_did_phase: cohort-vs-aggregate Honest-DiD sensitivity quotient frontier; q1_spectral_phase_transition: Markov event-window row-space threshold; both adjacent but not IF-equivalence frontiers

published_prior_art.cite: none; closest are BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024, ChenSantAnnaXie2025, and RothSantAnna2021Efficient, none stating the four-estimator quotient IF frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis: homogeneous-effect corner for staggered clean-basis coefficients, not the same four-estimator IF claim

published_prior_art.cite: SunAbraham2021 / CallawaySantAnna2021 / Wooldridge2021: homogeneous-effects equivalence is the standard sanity corner

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 block names CallawaySantAnna2021 as the closest paper, but BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 is closer for the variance/efficiency gap because it directly compares robust event-study alternatives to efficient imputation.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses RothSantAnna2021Efficient, which derives an efficient estimator in a class nesting popular staggered-rollout DiD estimators and is a closer efficiency-frontier comparator.

soundness_flags.label: Formal setup
soundness_flags.one_line: R_i is defined with cohort-dependent pre-treatment length, but Sigma^{-1/2}R_i is used as a common vector against Gamma_m tau; the common embedding/index set must be specified.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Var(delta_m-delta_m')>0 proves IF non-equivalence, but strict variance inequality also needs the norm difference term Var(delta_m)-Var(delta_m') plus 2Cov(psi_BJS,delta_m-delta_m'); the stated exception is incomplete.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Homogeneity only on cells with a_gt != 0 does not imply Gamma_m tau=0 if Gamma_m loads on zero-weight cohort-time cells; require homogeneity on all active Gamma columns or state Gamma_m annihilates those directions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Theorem 1's variance conclusion with the exact variance-difference identity, or restrict the strict-variance claim to BJS versus m under the stated orthogonality normalization.
Define a fixed residual coordinate system for R_i and Sigma, including never-treated and not-yet-treated cells, so Gamma_m tau and Sigma^{-1/2}R_i have compatible dimensions.
Strengthen Theorem 2 by assuming tau_gt is constant on every active Gamma_m column, or prove Gamma_m annihilates all zero-weight and constant-effect directions by construction.
Add RothSantAnna2021Efficient and the BJS precision-comparison result to §5 and explain why the proposed quotient frontier is not already implied by their efficient-estimator comparisons.

recommended_next_step: Patch the three soundness issues, then rerun novelty review with Roth-Sant'Anna efficient rollout added as a load-bearing comparator.
